utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 items are present. Checking each: health_coverage includes both physical and mental health insurance with respective costs and a total cost — fully specified. property_coverage includes home/contents insurance with cost — fully specified. travel_coverage includes travel insurance with cost — fully specified. additional_services is included with description and cost — fully specified. total_budget includes complete monthly and annual cost breakdown — fully specified. Therefore, all items are accounted for and meet the detail requirements.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING:
First, I identify each item in the final coverage plan and match it to the corresponding item in the ground-truth ratings. For health_coverage, the description says 'LifeFirst Standard Plan (Physical & Mental Health Combined)', so both the physical_health and mental_health components refer to the 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage. For physical_health, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' rating is 9; for mental_health, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' rating is also 9.
For property_coverage, the description is 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage (Home Contents with Adventure Equipment Focus)', which corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection.home_content with a rating of 9.
For travel_coverage, the description is 'LifeFirst Standard Plan (Adventure Activities and Frequent International Trips)', which matches 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with a rating of 9.
Additional_services is 'None', so no mapping is needed here. Only the three coverage items need mapping.
